text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST AND MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 64-year-old female with known right upper lobe pulmonary adenocarcinoma harboring EGFR L858R mutation, currently on maintenance pemetrexed and bevacizumab following completion of four cycles of carboplatin/pemetrexed/bevacizumab. History of prior osimertinib treatment (14-month duration) with disease progression and two treated brain metastases (stereotactic radiosurgery to right frontal lobe and right cerebellar hemisphere, both treated approximately one year prior). Routine restaging evaluation on maintenance therapy, cycle 6 of maintenance. Prior CT at approximately 2 months of maintenance demonstrated stable disease with right upper lobe mass measuring 2.2 cm.

TECHNIQUE:
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis: Multidetector CT performed following intravenous administration of 100 mL of Omnipaque 350 contrast material. Helical acquisition through the thorax, abdomen, and pelvis with multiplanar reformations in axial, coronal, and sagittal planes. Lung window, mediastinal window, and bone window images reviewed. Slice thickness 2.5 mm.

MRI brain: Multiparametric 3T MRI including pre- and post-gadolinium sequences. Sequences obtained: axial T1, axial T2, axial FLAIR, axial DWI/ADC, axial GRE/susceptibility-weighted imaging, pre-contrast sagittal T1 MPRAGE, and post-contrast axial and coronal T1 MPRAGE following intravenous administration of gadobutrol (Gadavist) 0.1 mmol/kg.

PRIOR STUDIES FOR COMPARISON:
- CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast, approximately 2 months prior (maintenance cycle 2): right upper lobe mass 2.2 cm, stable subcentimeter mediastinal nodes, no pleural effusion
- MRI brain with gadolinium, approximately 5 months prior (at initiation of maintenance): right frontal treated lesion 0.6 cm, right cerebellar treated lesion 0.4 cm, no new untreated brain lesions
- CT chest at commencement of maintenance, approximately 7 months prior from current exam: right upper lobe mass 2.4 cm after four cycles of platinum doublet

---

FINDINGS:

CT CHEST:

Lungs and Airways:
There has been interval growth of the known right upper lobe mass. The lesion currently measures approximately 3.8 cm in its longest axial dimension (previously 2.2 cm on the comparison study two months prior, and 2.4 cm at the start of maintenance approximately five months ago). The mass demonstrates a heterogeneous soft tissue attenuation with irregular, somewhat spiculated margins. The lesion abuts but does not clearly obstruct the right upper lobe bronchus, though there is mild narrowing of the lumen at the point of contact. No endobronchial component is definitively identified. Mild post-obstructive atelectasis is present within the right upper lobe lateral segment. The mass does not appear to invade the chest wall or the adjacent mediastinal structures on current examination, though the mediastinal fat plane at the level of the right paratracheal region appears less distinct than on prior imaging.

Additionally, there are two new pulmonary nodules not present on prior imaging:
- A new solid nodule in the right lower lobe medial basilar segment measuring approximately 0.8 cm in greatest dimension, with well-defined margins and soft tissue density without internal calcification or satellite lesions.
- A new solid nodule in the left lower lobe posterior basilar segment measuring approximately 0.6 cm, similarly well-defined and solid in attenuation.

These two new foci are in locations remote from the primary site and without an alternate benign explanation given the clinical context. No additional pulmonary nodules are identified. No ground-glass opacities or consolidation to suggest superimposed infection or drug-related pneumonitis. No pneumothorax.

Pleurae:
No pleural effusion is identified bilaterally. Previously noted small right pleural effusion (which resolved after three cycles of platinum doublet) remains absent. No pleural thickening or pleural-based nodularity.

Mediastinum and Hila:
There is a mildly enlarged right paratracheal lymph node at station 4R, measuring approximately 1.4 cm in short axis, increased from a subcentimeter measurement on the prior comparison scan. On the study from cycle 1 of maintenance (approximately five months ago), station 4R measured less than 1 cm. The current measurement represents a return to lymph node enlargement in a station previously involved at initial diagnosis. Station 7 (subcarinal) lymph node measures 0.7 cm in short axis, not meeting threshold for pathologic enlargement but mildly prominent. Left paratracheal and left hilar lymph nodes are not enlarged. No anterior mediastinal, posterior mediastinal, or prevascular adenopathy identified.

Heart and Pericardium:
Cardiac size is within normal limits. No pericardial effusion. The aorta and main pulmonary artery are normal in caliber.

Chest Wall and Soft Tissues:
No chest wall invasion identified. No osseous lesions within the visualized ribs, sternum, or thoracic spine. Soft tissues are unremarkable.

Upper Abdomen (CT with contrast):

Liver:
The liver is normal in size with a smooth contour. Hepatic parenchyma demonstrates homogeneous enhancement without focal hepatic lesions, masses, or areas suspicious for metastatic involvement. No biliary ductal dilatation. Portal vein and hepatic veins are patent.

Gallbladder and Bile Ducts:
The gallbladder is surgically absent (confirmed on prior studies). The common bile duct is not dilated.

Pancreas:
The pancreas is normal in size, morphology, and enhancement pattern. No pancreatic ductal dilatation. No focal pancreatic lesion.

Spleen:
The spleen is normal in size and attenuation. No focal splenic lesion.

Adrenal Glands:
Bilateral adrenal glands are unremarkable. No adrenal mass or nodule identified. Previously normal adrenal glands remain so on current examination.

Kidneys and Ureters:
Bilateral kidneys are normal in size with symmetric cortical enhancement and excretion. No hydronephrosis. No renal mass. No urolithiasis identified on the current examination. A 0.4 cm simple-appearing hypodense cortical cyst is noted in the upper pole of the right kidney, unchanged from prior and consistent with a benign renal cyst (Bosniak I).

Pelvis (CT with contrast):

Bladder:
The urinary bladder is well-distended with normal wall thickness. No intraluminal filling defect.

Female Pelvis:
Uterus is not visualized (prior hysterectomy per prior examination reports). Ovarian tissue is not identified. No pelvic free fluid. No pelvic lymphadenopathy meeting size criteria for pathologic enlargement.

Bowel:
Visualized bowel loops are within normal limits without evidence of obstruction, perforation, or wall thickening. No mesenteric or retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy.

Bones (visualized skeleton):
No osseous lesions within the thoracic, lumbar, or visualized pelvic bones to suggest skeletal metastatic disease. No pathologic fractures. Mild degenerative changes of the lower lumbar spine (L4-L5 and L5-S1 levels) consistent with spondylosis, age-appropriate.

---

MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM:

Supratentorial Structures:
Brain parenchyma demonstrates normal signal intensity on T1 and T2 sequences without evidence of new parenchymal signal abnormality to suggest new metastatic deposits, infarct, or demyelination in the visualized cortical and subcortical regions.

Right Frontal Lobe - SRS-Treated Lesion:
At the site of the previously SRS-treated right frontal lobe lesion, there is now a focus of T1 hypointensity and T2/FLAIR hyperintensity measuring approximately 1.4 cm in greatest dimension, with surrounding FLAIR signal hyperintensity (vasogenic edema pattern) extending into the adjacent white matter over a zone of approximately 2.5 cm. On post-contrast T1 sequences, there is heterogeneous nodular rim enhancement at this site, increased compared with the prior MRI at the time of maintenance initiation when this lesion measured 0.6 cm and demonstrated only faint residual enhancement. The surrounding edema is also substantially increased from prior. There is no evidence of hemorrhage on susceptibility-weighted sequences at this site.

This combination of lesion enlargement and increased enhancement in the context of the prior treatment site raises concern for either local tumor recurrence or late radiation necrosis; these entities cannot be reliably distinguished on conventional MRI sequences alone. Clinical and perfusion imaging correlation is recommended.

Right Cerebellar Hemisphere - SRS-Treated Lesion:
The previously treated right cerebellar hemispheric lesion is again identified. It measures approximately 0.5 cm (previously 0.4 cm), demonstrating minimal punctate post-contrast enhancement consistent with chronic post-treatment change. No significant surrounding edema. No substantive interval change. This appearance is most consistent with treated/involuting metastasis.

Infratentorial Structures:
No new enhancing lesions identified in the cerebellar hemispheres, vermis, or brainstem. The fourth ventricle is patent and not displaced. No cerebellar tonsillar herniation.

Ventricles and Cisterns:
The lateral ventricles, third and fourth ventricles are within normal limits in size. Mild prominence of the frontal horn of the right lateral ventricle is noted, possibly reflective of adjacent white matter edema from the right frontal lesion, rather than true hydrocephalus. Basal cisterns are widely patent.

Calvarial and Skull Base:
No calvarial lesions or bony erosion identified. No leptomeningeal enhancement on post-contrast sequences. The skull base appears intact.

Vascular Structures:
Major intracranial vessels are patent on the time-of-flight MRA sequences obtained. No aneurysm, arteriovenous malformation, or dural sinus thrombosis.

Soft Tissues:
Normal-appearing scalp and soft tissues without evidence of subcutaneous metastasis.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. DISEASE PROGRESSION IN THE CHEST: The right upper lobe primary mass has grown substantially, now measuring 3.8 cm compared with 2.2 cm on the prior study from two months ago, representing an approximately 73% increase from the maintenance nadir of 2.2 cm and a 58% increase from the 2.4 cm measurement at maintenance initiation. This degree of interval growth is consistent with loss of disease control on maintenance pemetrexed/bevacizumab.

2. NEW BILATERAL PULMONARY NODULES: Two new solid pulmonary nodules are identified -- a 0.8 cm nodule in the right lower lobe and a 0.6 cm nodule in the left lower lobe -- both new from the prior examination. Given the clinical context of active EGFR-mutant pulmonary adenocarcinoma, these are highly suspicious for intrapulmonary metastatic deposits, representing new sites of intrathoracic disease spread.

3. RECURRENT RIGHT PARATRACHEAL ADENOPATHY: The right paratracheal (station 4R) lymph node has re-enlarged to 1.4 cm in short axis, returning to a size consistent with pathologic involvement. This represents nodal disease recurrence at a previously involved mediastinal station.

4. ENLARGING RIGHT FRONTAL LESION WITH INCREASED EDEMA: The previously SRS-treated right frontal lesion has grown from 0.6 cm to 1.4 cm with new surrounding vasogenic edema and heterogeneous enhancement. The differential diagnosis includes local tumor recurrence versus radiation necrosis; dedicated MRI perfusion and spectroscopy are recommended to further characterize this lesion given the clinical implications for management.

5. STABLE RIGHT CEREBELLAR TREATED LESION: The right cerebellar metastasis treated with SRS remains stable and appears consistent with treated disease.

6. NO NEW UNTREATED BRAIN METASTASES: No additional enhancing intracranial lesions are identified beyond the known treated sites.

7. NO HEPATIC, ADRENAL, OR SKELETAL METASTASES: The abdomen, pelvis, and visualized osseous structures remain without evidence of distant organ metastases on the current examination.

Overall, the findings are indicative of systemic disease progression involving the right upper lobe primary tumor, new bilateral pulmonary nodules consistent with intrapulmonary metastatic spread, and recurrent mediastinal nodal involvement. Concurrent intracranial concern for enlarging treated right frontal lesion requiring further characterization. These findings indicate loss of disease control on maintenance pemetrexed/bevacizumab.

Results communicated to the treating oncology team at time of interpretation.
